info.title: Footstep API
info.description: Terrain-intelligent routing, geocoding, elevation, and spatial analysis. Every routing response includes terrain analytics by default. All responses use self-documenting field names (distance_meters, duration_seconds, grade_percent) and support two formats: footstep (default, compact) and geojson (standard GeoJSON).

post.summary: Calculate a route with terrain analysis
post.description: Calculate a route between two or more locations. Returns turn-by-turn directions with terrain analytics (ascent, descent, grade, difficulty) included by default. All distances are in meters, durations in seconds.

schema.description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection where each leg is a Feature with LineString geometry. Route-level summary in top-level properties.

post.summary: Get elevation data for points or a path
post.description: Query elevation values for coordinates or an encoded polyline. Returns height values with a computed summary including ascent, descent, min/max/avg elevation.

schema.description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection of Point features with 3D coordinates [lon, lat, elevation].

post.summary: Optimise visit order for multiple stops
post.description: Find the optimal order to visit multiple locations. Returns the optimised route with terrain analytics and savings (distance and time) compared to the original order.

schema.description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection of the optimised route. Same data as footstep format, different shape.

post.summary: Snap a GPS trace to the road network
post.description: Map-match a GPS trace to the road network. Returns a snapped polyline with confidence score. Optionally includes directions with terrain analytics, road attributes (surface, grade, road class), and per-point match quality.

200.description: Snapped trace with optional route and attributes

post.description: Generate time or distance-based reachability contours from a location. Default format returns structured contours with geometry. Use format=geojson for a standard GeoJSON FeatureCollection. Supports terrain-aware reachability via travel_options.

schema.description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection with polygon features for each contour. Properties include contour value, metric, and color.

post.description: Compute a time and distance matrix between sources and targets. Returns a 2D matrix indexed by [source][target] with distance_meters and duration_seconds for each pair. Null cells indicate unreachable pairs. All distances are in meters regardless of request units.

schema.description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection where each source-target pair is a Feature with straight-line LineString geometry. Properties include distance_meters, duration_seconds, and source/target indices.

post.summary: Compare travel modes between two locations
post.description: Calculate routes between the same locations using multiple travel modes and return a side-by-side comparison. Includes distance, duration, and terrain difficulty for each mode, plus a summary showing which is fastest, shortest, and easiest. Useful for deciding how to travel.

200.description: Routes for each travel mode with comparison

post.summary: Geocode a destination and route to it
post.description: Find a place by name or address, then calculate a route to it from your origin. Combines geocoding and routing in a single call. Returns the matched destination, the full route with terrain analytics, and alternative geocoding matches.

400.description: Invalid request or destination not found

post.summary: Snap coordinates to the nearest road
post.description: Check if coordinates are on a routable road and get the nearest snapped position. Returns distance, on-road status, and optionally edge metadata (road class, surface, speed).

basic.summary: Check if points are on roads

schema.description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection of Point features for each located point.

post.description: Calculate a route between locations and search for places along the corridor. Results are ranked by how little detour they add. Useful for finding stops (petrol stations, restaurants, charging points) on the way to a destination.

200.description: Route with places found along the corridor

get.summary: Search for a place or address
get.description: Forward geocode: convert a text query (address, place name, landmark) into geographic coordinates. Results are ranked by relevance. Use focus.point to bias results towards a location. Use boundary.country to restrict to specific countries.

parameters.name: boundary.country
parameters.description: Comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g. GBR,USA)

parameters.name: layers
parameters.description: Comma-separated layers to filter: venue, address, street, locality, region, country

results.label: 10 Downing Street, London, England, United Kingdom
results.name: 10 Downing Street

get.description: Reverse geocode: convert geographic coordinates into a human-readable address or place name. Returns the nearest matching locations sorted by proximity.

parameters.name: point.lat
parameters.description: Latitude of the point to reverse geocode

parameters.name: point.lon
parameters.description: Longitude of the point to reverse geocode

parameters.name: layers
parameters.description: Comma-separated layers to filter: venue, address, street, locality, region, country

results.label: 10 Downing Street, London, England, United Kingdom
results.name: 10 Downing Street

get.description: Typeahead search: get suggestions as the user types. Optimised for low-latency responses. Use focus.point to bias results towards the user's current location.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Partial text input for typeahead suggestions

parameters.name: boundary.country
parameters.description: Comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g. GBR,USA)

parameters.name: layers
parameters.description: Comma-separated layers to filter: venue, address, street, locality, region, country

results.label: Buckingham Palace, London, England, United Kingdom
results.name: Buckingham Palace

results.label: Empire State Building, New York, United States
results.name: Empire State Building

get.description: Forward geocode using structured address components instead of free text. More precise than text search when you already have address fields separated (e.g. from a database or form). Provide as many fields as you have. The more you provide, the more accurate the result.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: House number and street (e.g. '10 Downing Street')

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g. GBR, USA)

parameters.name: layers
parameters.description: Comma-separated layers to filter: venue, address, street, locality, region, country

results.label: 10 Downing Street, London, England, United Kingdom
results.name: 10 Downing Street

post.summary: Geocode up to 1,000 addresses in one request
post.description: Batch geocode a list of addresses in a single request. Each item can be free text or structured address components. Results are returned per-item with the original index preserved, so failed items don't break the batch. Use this for spreadsheet imports, database enrichment, or any bulk geocoding task.

results.label: Buckingham Palace, London, England, United Kingdom
results.name: Buckingham Palace

get.summary: Search for places and points of interest
get.description: Search for places by category or keyword near a location. Unlike the text search endpoint (which finds specific addresses and named places), this is designed for discovery queries like 'coffee shops near me' or 'restaurants in Soho'. Results are ranked by proximity and relevance.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: What to search for (e.g. 'coffee shop', 'restaurant', 'supermarket', 'train station', 'park')

parameters.name: boundary.country
parameters.description: Comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g. GBR,USA)

results.label: Caravan, Granary Square, London, England, United Kingdom
results.name: Caravan

get.summary: Parse a free-text address into components
get.description: Break a free-text address into structured components (house number, road, city, state, postcode, country). Use this to normalise messy address data before passing it to structured search, or to understand what parts of an address were recognised.

value.input: 10 Downing Street, London SW1A 2AA, United Kingdom

post.description: Accept a natural language query and return structured geospatial results. The endpoint classifies intent (route, geocode, isochrone, etc.), extracts locations and parameters, calls the appropriate backend, and returns the full result with a natural language summary. Use context.location to help resolve relative references like 'nearest' or 'nearby'.

value.query: Walking route from Kings Cross to Tower Bridge
How far can I cycle in 20 minutes from Liverpool Street station?
Directions to the nearest train station

200.description: Structured result from the resolved query

post.description: Parse, correct, and standardize addresses using AI. Fixes typos, expands abbreviations, infers missing components (postcode, region, country), and returns structured address components with a confidence score. Each correction is itemised so you can see exactly what changed.

Format.description: Response format. footstep = optimised for app developers (encoded polyline, flat structure). geojson = standard GeoJSON FeatureCollection (decoded coordinates, immediately usable in Leaflet/Mapbox GL/deck.gl).

TravelOptions.description: Fine-tuning options. Include only the key matching your travel type.

begin_shape_index.description: Index into the leg shape where this step starts

end_shape_index.description: Index into the leg shape where this step ends

travel_mode.description: Mode of travel: drive, pedestrian, bicycle, transit

bearing_before.description: Bearing in degrees approaching the step (0-360)

bearing_after.description: Bearing in degrees leaving the step (0-360)

roundabout_exit_count.description: Which exit to take in a roundabout

TerrainSummary.description: Terrain analytics computed from elevation data. Included automatically in routing responses.

difficulty.description: Terrain difficulty classification based on grade distribution

original_index.description: Original input index (present in optimise responses)

Leg.description: A route leg between two waypoints

Route.description: A complete route with terrain analytics

units.description: Echo of request units (informational only, response values are always in meters/seconds)

narrative.description: Natural language summary of the route including distance, duration, terrain, and key landmarks. Present when narrative=true in the request.

travel.description: How to travel: auto (car), pedestrian, bicycle, bus, or truck

narrative.description: Include a natural language summary of the route. Uses AI generation, adds ~500ms latency.

alternates.description: Alternate routes (same shape as primary route)

narrative.description: Include a natural language summary of the optimised route. Uses AI generation, adds ~500ms latency.

optimized_order.description: Original location indices in optimised visit order

savings.description: Savings compared to original location order

output.description: route = directions with terrain, attributes = road details, both = combined

route.description: Present when output includes 'route'. Includes terrain analytics.

edges.description: Present when output includes 'attributes'. Road segment details.

surface.description: Road surface type (paved_smooth, paved, gravel, etc.)

distance_from_trace_meters.description: Distance from original trace point in meters

IsochroneResponse.description: Structured isochrone response with typed contours

metric.description: Whether this contour is time or distance based

matrix.description: 2D matrix indexed as matrix[source_index][target_index]. null = unreachable pair.

snapped.description: Snapped position (absent if not near road)

distance_meters.description: Distance to nearest road in meters

label.description: Full human-readable label (e.g. 'London, England, United Kingdom')

layer.description: Result layer: venue, address, street, locality, region, country

source.description: Data source: whosonfirst, openstreetmap, openaddresses, geonames

items.description: Addresses to geocode. Each item can use free text (text field) or structured components (address, locality, country, etc.).

error.description: Error message if this item failed

matched.description: Items with at least one result

components.description: Parsed address components. Only fields that were detected are included.

query.description: Natural language query (e.g. 'Walking route from Kings Cross to Tower Bridge', 'Where is Buckingham Palace?', 'How far can I drive in 30 minutes from Liverpool Street?')

context.description: Optional context to improve query understanding

location.description: User's current location. Helps resolve relative references like 'nearest' or 'nearby'.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code to bias geocoding (e.g. GBR, USA)

parameters.description: Parameters inferred from the query and passed to the backend endpoint

result.description: Full response from the backend endpoint (RouteResponse, GeocodingSearchResponse, IsochroneResponse, etc.)

summary.description: Natural language summary of the result

text.description: Free-text address (can be messy, incomplete, or contain typos)

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code hint to improve parsing accuracy

corrections.description: List of corrections applied. Empty array if input was clean.

original.description: Original value (null if inferred from nothing)

type.description: What kind of correction was applied

routes.description: Routes keyed by travel mode (e.g. { "pedestrian": Route, "bicycle": Route })

fastest.description: Travel mode with the shortest duration

shortest.description: Travel mode with the shortest distance

easiest.description: Travel mode with the lowest terrain difficulty

summary.description: Natural language comparison of the routes

destination.description: Place name, address, or landmark to find and route to

destination_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code hint for geocoding (e.g. GBR, USA)

narrative.description: Include a natural language route summary. Adds ~500ms.

destination.description: The geocoded destination that was routed to

route.description: Route from origin to the geocoded destination

alternatives.description: Other geocoding matches (top 3) in case the primary result wasn't what you meant

distance_meters.description: Distance from the search point in meters

search.description: What to search for along the route (e.g. 'coffee shop', 'petrol station', 'supermarket')

max_detour_seconds.description: Maximum acceptable detour in seconds (1 min to 1 hour)

size.description: Maximum number of places to return

route.description: The original route between the locations

places.description: Places found along the route, ranked by detour time

detour_seconds.description: How many seconds of detour this stop adds

detour_meters.description: How many meters of detour this stop adds

position_along_route_percent.description: Where along the route this place is (0 = start, 100 = end)
